game_theme.title: Measurement Conversion and Data
game_theme.mission_intro: Use measurement conversions, word-problem models, line plots, area, and perimeter to help the Skill World crew organize a festival map.

lesson.story_mission: The crew must label supplies, paths, and data displays with correct measurements.
lesson.mini_lesson: Convert larger units to smaller units by multiplying. For data, read every fraction label on a line plot. For rectangles, area covers the inside and perimeter measures the outside path.
lesson.objective: Convert measurements, solve measurement problems, and interpret line plots with fractions.
lesson.concept_explanation: Grade 4 measurement problems connect units, operations, data, and rectangle models. The unit label tells what the number means.
lesson.common_misconception: Students may divide when converting larger units to smaller units, confuse units, misread fraction labels, or mix up area and perimeter.
lesson.real_world_connection: These skills help with recipes, maps, building projects, sports fields, and science data.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read the unit, choose the operation, use the model, and answer with the correct label.

audio.text: In this lesson, convert units, read fractional line plots, and distinguish area from perimeter with careful labels.

misconception_bank.unit_conversion_direction_error: Converts in the wrong direction or divides instead of multiplying.
misconception_bank.measurement_unit_confusion: Uses the wrong unit label or conversion factor.
misconception_bank.line_plot_fraction_error: Misreads fractional labels or counts X marks incorrectly.
misconception_bank.area_perimeter_confusion: Uses area when perimeter is needed or perimeter when area is needed.

questions.prompt: A recipe needs 2 quarts of juice. The pitcher has 9 cups. How many cups are left after the recipe?
hints: Read the problem and name what is being asked.
Use the visual model to organize the quantities or attributes.
Check that your answer includes the correct value and label.

feedback.correct: Yes! Your answer matches the model and the math relationship.
feedback.incorrect: Try again. Reread the prompt and use the labels in the model.

story.text: Read this page to enter Measurement Conversion and Data. The crew has a real Grade 4 problem to solve, so notice the quantities, labels, and math words before you start. Think about what the question is asking and how a model can keep your work organized.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: Read this page for the main lesson on convert measurements, solve measurement problems, and interpret line plots with fractions. Use precise vocabulary, name the units or geometry attributes, and explain why each step makes sense. If a number, line, angle, or shape label matters, say it in your own words before answering.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: Read this page while you watch the worked example. The model is not decoration; it shows how to connect the problem words to a mathematical relationship. Pause on each label and ask what it tells you to calculate, compare, measure, or classify.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: Read this page before the demo. Follow the first move, then predict the next move before checking the example. Good mathematicians use the visual evidence and the answer sentence together, not just a quick guess.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: Read this page to practice with support. Press Read Question for each problem so you hear the full math wording clearly. Then use the model, choose or type your answer, and check whether your response includes the correct value, unit, or geometry word.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: Read this page for the challenge round. These problems may mix vocabulary, diagrams, and computation, so slow down and identify the exact relationship first. Press Read Question when you need the prompt spoken again, then explain your reasoning.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: Read this page for the checkpoint. This is your chance to show independent skill with the same careful habits: read the prompt, use the visual, answer with the correct label, and review your work. Use Read Question to hear each checkpoint item.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: Read this page to celebrate your progress. The badge means you practiced the lesson, watched models, tried challenges, and checked your understanding. Name one strategy you used today so it is ready for the next math mission.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: Read this page to save your growth profile. Look at what you completed and notice which kinds of questions felt strongest. Your next practice should build on those strengths and revisit any unit labels, data labels, angle measures, or shape attributes that still need attention.
profile.label: Read This Page
